subject: Change bbs-e-p baseOS img to integration-
commit_message:
Change bbs-e-p baseOS img to integration-

Benefits from switching over:
* minimal {java11,python} images maintained by integration team
* using currently "blessed by seccom" versions (:latest tag used)
* should limit spread of legal issues across layers
* integration images will be the first to have automated compliance
  documentation
* should limit spread of base layers (contributing to deployment
  footprint - more base layers = more to download, more to store etc...)

Issue-ID: INT-1864
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2420
Signed-off-by: Alexander Mazuruk <a.mazuruk@samsung.com>
Change-Id: I48f6dda04e8a31bf175501e82aa776aba34aba72

Patch Set 10:

(4 comments)

I managed to run csit for bss-event-processor and pmsh- same results with both nexus and my localhost registry (with images from this patch). Didnt find csit for datalake-handler, please let me know if there are any.

pmsh runs with py3.9 now, which I switched to be tested in tox. There are some deprecation warnings but it seems it runs fine

img sizes:
pmsh lost half of its weight: 156MB->82.6
datalake feeder got a bit heavier: 570->641
datalake exposure service got a bit lighter: 785->628
bbs didnt change 262->194

for bbs & datalake i bumped patch version only (unsepcified java11 to java11.0.9)

Patch Set 10:

> Patch Set 10:
> 
> (4 comments)
> 
> I managed to run csit for bss-event-processor and pmsh- same results with both nexus and my localhost registry (with images from this patch). Didnt find csit for datalake-handler, please let me know if there are any.
> 
> pmsh runs with py3.9 now, which I switched to be tested in tox. There are some deprecation warnings but it seems it runs fine
> 
> img sizes:
> pmsh lost half of its weight: 156MB->82.6
> datalake feeder got a bit heavier: 570->641
> datalake exposure service got a bit lighter: 785->628
> bbs didnt change 262->194
> 
> for bbs & datalake i bumped patch version only (unsepcified java11 to java11.0.9)

while for pmsh i bumped minor version as python got bumped to 3.9
I'm unsure if i should've bumped version in the repo root pom.xml?

> 
> while for pmsh i bumped minor version as python got bumped to 3.9
> I'm unsure if i should've bumped version in the repo root pom.xml?

root pom.xml version bump is optional - it has no effect for sub-component builds/versioning

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

Recommend separate submission for bbs, datalake and pmSH as these separate sub-components/services with no relations (other than just sharing common repository). This would allow backtrace/revert any change in future if needed
